air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.

ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.

artist: noun. A person who creates art.

astir: adjective. In motion; characterized by motion.
astrict: verb. To bind, constrain, or restrict.
att: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Lao kip.

car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
cart: noun. A small, open, wheeled vehicle, drawn or pushed by a person or animal, often with two wheels on one axle, more often used for
cast: verb. To move, or be moved, away.
cat: noun. A mammal of the family Felidae.
cis: adjective. Having two mutations on two genes on the same chromosome of a homologous pair.
cist: noun. A small receptacle for sacred utensils carried in festivals in Ancient Greece.
crista: noun. Any of the internal compartments formed by the inner membrane of a mitochondrion, where many chemical reactions take place.
iatric: adjective. Of or pertaining to a physician or physicians.
iris: noun. A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant) on Wikipedia.

ras: noun. An Ethiopian king or prince.

ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.

sat: verb. simple past and past participle of sit
satiric: adjective. Of or pertaining to satire.
scar: noun. A permanent mark on the skin, sometimes caused by the healing of a wound.
scatt: noun. Obsolete spelling of scat (“tax, tribute”).
sic: adverb. Thus; as written; used to indicate, for example, that text is being quoted as it is from the source.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
sitar: noun. A Hindustani / Indian classical stringed instrument, typically having a gourd as its resonating chamber.

stair: noun. A single step in a staircase.
star: noun. Any small, natural and bright dot in the sky, most visible in the night or twilight sky.
start: noun. The beginning of an activity.
static: adjective. Unchanging; that cannot or does not change.
stir: verb. To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
strait: adjective. Narrow; restricted as to space or room; close.
stria: noun. A stripe, usually one of a set of parallel stripes.
tacit: adjective. Implied, but not made explicit, especially through silence.
tact: noun. Sensitive mental touch; special skill or faculty; keen perception or discernment; ready power of appreciating and doing what is
tar: noun. A black, oil, sticky, viscous substance, consisting mainly of hydrocarbons derived from organic materials such as wood, peat, or coal.
tart: adjective. Sharp to the taste; acid; sour.
tat: noun. Cheap and vulgar tastelessness; sleaze.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.

tit: noun. A person's breast or nipple.

trait: noun. An identifying characteristic, habit or trend.

arist: noun. A rising, as from a seat, a bed, or the ground, or from below the horizon.

crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
rais: noun. Alternative form of reis (“holder of military rank”).
ratti: noun. A traditional Indian unit of measure for mass, approximately 0.1215 grams.

saic: noun. A kind of ketch heavily used in the Black Sea, Tisa, Danube and Sava
sart: noun. An assart, or clearing; land cleared for agriculture.
